questions.question: What are the rules for smoking in aircraft?

Smoking is not permitted during take-off or landing; or whenever the pilot in command has directed not to smoke
Smoking is not permitted during take-off and landing however electronic cigarettes are permitted

questions.question: Can a passenger be seated in a control seat?
options: Yes, but only if there are no other passengers onboard
Yes provided that the pilot is satisifed that they will not interfere with the controls and that two way communication exists are all times
Yes, but the other passengers must be satisifed with this arrangement

questions.question: What is the minimum height a helicopter can fly over a populus area or a public gathering?
options: You must not fly a rotorcraft over a populous area or public gathering below 1,000 ft above the highest feature or obstacle within a horizontal radius of 300 m of the point on the ground
You must not fly a rotorcraft over a populous area or public gathering below 1,000 ft above the highest feature or obstacle within a horizontal radius of 600 m of the point on the ground
You must not fly a rotorcraft over a populous area or public gathering below 500 ft above the highest feature or obstacle within a horizontal radius of 300 m of the point on the ground
questions.kd: Unit 1.5.8   2.3.7(b) flights over public gatherings

questions.question: You are about to depart but cannot obtain your destination forecast for another 20 minutes. Your departure aerodrome is currently VMC but forecast to deteriorate in 40 minutes. Under CASA regulations, can you still depart?
options: Yes, provided you expect to arrive at the destination before weather deterioration
No, you must have the destination forecast before departure
Yes, as long as the departure aerodrome is VMC at time of departure
No, flights are prohibited if destination forecast is unavailable regardless of departure conditions

questions.question: If a required piece of equipment becomes unserviceable during flight, what is the pilot’s immediate responsibility?

Notify ATC and proceed as planned
Land as soon as practicable and record the defect in the maintenance release
Attempt to repair the equipment in-flight

An entry must be made including date, signature, and licence number

questions.question: A forecast for your destination must be valid for:
options: 60 minutes before to 60 minutes after your ETA
30 minutes before to 60 minutes after your ETA
60 minutes before to 30 minutes after your ETA
30 minutes before to 30 minutes after your ETA

questions.question: You plan to depart Bairnsdale (YBNS) for Moruya on a private flight but you are unable to obtain a weather forecast. Are you permitted to depart?
options: Yes, as it is a private flight
No, you must obtain a weather forecast
Yes, provided you are saitsifed the weather at Bairnsdale will permit a safe landing within an hour of deaparture, and can obtain a forecast within 30 minutes of departure
No, as the flight is beyond 100nm and a weather forecast must be obtained

options: Quebec November Hotel, one zero one three

questions.question: You must you plan for an alternate aerodrome when the ETA to your destination is has below minima weather on the TAF:
options: 60 minutes before to 60 minutes after your ETA
30 minutes before to 60 minutes after your ETA
60 minutes before to 30 minutes after your ETA
30 minutes before to 30 minutes after your ETA
questions.kd: Unit 1.5.8   2.4.1(b) aerodrome meteorological minima

questions.question: What are the alternate minimas for a non controlled aerodrome?
options: Not more than SCT cloud at 1500ft, and visbility greater than 8km
Not more than SCT cloud at 1000, and visbility greater than 3km
Not more than SCT cloud at 1500ft, and visbility greater than 5km
Not more than SCT cloud at 1000ft, and visbility greater than 8km

questions.question: What determines the serviceability of an altimetre for an air transport VFR flight?
options: It must be within 30ft with an accurate QNH setting
It must be within 50ft with an accurate QNH setting
It must be within 60ft with an accurate QNH setting, or 75ft if test site is above 3000ft AMSL
It must be within 100ft with an accurate QNH setting, or 110ft if test site is above 3000ft AMSL

questions.question: You are about to depart on a single pilot Day VFR air transport flight and you notice that one of your two altimetres fitted is beyond its serviceability limits. Are you permitted to take off?

Yes, however it must be placarded unserviceable and a note made in the maintenance release
Yes, provided you remain within 50nm of departure aerodrome

120 minutes before entry into controlled airspace
